result.markdown: --- date: last-modified date-format: "YYYY/MM/DD" title: "29. Proportions of the Normal Curve" subtitle: "One-Tailed Reference Table and Reproducible Generator" reference-location: margin resources: - tables/make_normal_tail_table.R - tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.csv - tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.tex - tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.pdf --- ::: {.cell} ::: This page reproduces the classic one-tailed normal table. Each cell gives the right-tail probability $$P(Z \ge z)$$ for a standard normal deviate, where the row gives the first decimal place and the column gives the second decimal place. For example, the entry at row `1.5` and column `1` corresponds to $z = 1.51$ and gives `0.0655`. The source files are available here: - [R script](tables/make_normal_tail_table.R) - [CSV values](tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.csv) - [LaTeX source](tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.tex) - [PDF table](tables/normal_curve_tail_proportions.pdf) The PDF below is produced by the R script and typeset in LaTeX. ```{=html} ```
